title: Inorganic membrane filtration articles and methods thereof
abstract: An inorganic membrane filtration article and methods for making the same. The membrane filtration article includes a sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb with a plurality of partition walls defining a plurality of open channels from an inlet end of the honeycomb to an outlet end of the honeycomb. The honeycomb is formed from a cordierite composition with low-sodium and/or low-potassium content for enhanced filtration performance.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A membrane filtration article comprising: a sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb comprising a plurality of porous partition walls defining a plurality of open channels, the open channels extend in an axial direction from an inlet end to an outlet end of the honeycomb, the plurality of partition walls include a composition comprising: AI 2 O3 32-38 wt.%; Si0 2 47-53 wt.%; MgO 10-16 wt.%; and Na 2 0 0.01-0.10 wt.%, and wherein at least 95 wt.% of the composition of the porous partition walls is a cordierite crystalline phase. 2. The article of claim 1 wherein the composition of the porous partition walls includes K 2 0 0.01-0.03 wt.%. 3. The article of claim 1 or claim 2 wherein the composition of the porous partition walls includes CaO 0.01-0.15 wt.%; Fe 2 0 3 0.4-1.0 wt.%; NiO 0.001 -0.01 wt.%; and Ti0 2 0.1-0.5 wt.%. 4. The article of any one of the preceding claims wherein the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb includes from 0.5 to 31 open channels per square centimeter of the inlet end. 5. The article of any one of the preceding claims wherein at least one of the porous partition walls have a thickness from 0.1 mm to 2.5 mm. 6. The article of any one of the preceding claims wherein the porous partition walls have pore diameters from about 1 micron to about 15 microns. 7. The article of any one of the preceding claims wherein the porous partition walls of the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb include a median pore diameter D50 from 2 microns to 10 microns. 8. The article of any one of the preceding claims further comprising a porous membrane layer on at least a portion of the porous partition walls, the porous membrane layer comprises at least 50 wt.% of a composition comprising: 

 AI 2 O3 31-37 wt.%; Si0 2 45-51 wt.%; MgO 10-16 wt.%; and (Na 2 0 + K 2 0) 0.01-0.10 wt.%, and wherein at least 95 wt.% of the composition of the porous membrane layer is a cordierite crystalline phase. 9. The article of claim 8 wherein the composition of the porous membrane layer includes CaO 0.05-0.15 wt.%; Fe 2 0 3 0.3-1.0 wt.%; NiO 0.001-0.1 wt.%; and Ti0 2 0.2-0.8 wt.%. 10. The article of claim 8 or claim 9 wherein the composition of the porous membrane layer includes Na 2 0 0.01 -0.7 wt.% and K 2 0 0.01-0.03 wt.%. 11. The article of any one of claims 8-10 wherein the composition of the porous membrane layer includes 1 wt. % to 50 wt. % alumina. 12. The article of any one of claims 8-11 wherein the porous membrane layer includes a median thickness from about 5 microns to about 25 microns. 13. The article of any one of claims 8-12 wherein the porous membrane layer includes pore diameters from about 0.1 micron to about 5 microns. 14. The article of any one of claims 8-13 wherein the porous membrane layer includes a median pore diameter D50 from about 0.2 microns to about 3 microns. 15. The article of any one of claims 8-14 wherein the porous membrane layer includes a porosity from about 40% to about 60%. 16. The article of any one of claims 8-15 wherein the porous partition walls of the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb having the porous membrane layer thereon have a water permeance from about 300 liters/hour/m 2 /bar to about 3,000 liters/hour/m 2 /bar. 17. A membrane filtration article comprising: a sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb comprising a plurality of porous partition walls defining a plurality of open channels, the open channels extend in an axial direction from an inlet end to an outlet end of the honeycomb, 

 the plurality of partition walls includes a composition comprising: AI 2 O3 32- 38 wt.%; Si0 2 47-53 wt.%; MgO 10-16 wt.%; and Na 2 0 0.01-0.10 wt.%, and wherein at least 97 wt.% of the composition of the porous partition walls is a cordierite crystalline phase. 18. The article of claim 17 further comprising a porous membrane layer on at least a portion of the porous partition walls, the porous membrane layer comprises at least 50 wt.% of a composition comprising: AI 2 O3 31-37 wt.%; S1O 2 45-51 wt.%; MgO 10-16 wt.%; and Na 2 0 + K 2 0 0.01 -0.10 wt.%, and wherein at least 97 wt.% of the composition of the porous membrane layer composition is the cordierite crystalline phase. 19. The article of claim 18 wherein the porous membrane layer further comprises from 1 wt. % to 50 wt. % alumina. 20. A method of making the membrane filtration article of claim 1, the method comprising: extruding a low-sodium cordierite precursor composition into a green body; sintering the green body to form the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb, contacting the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb and a low-sodium cordierite precursor composition to form a green membrane layer on at least a portion of the porous partition walls of the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb, firing the green membrane layer on the sintered flow-through ceramic honeycomb walls at from about 1 100Â°C to about 1300Â°C to form the sintered, porous membrane layer on the porous partition walls. 21. A method of using the membrane filtration article of claim 1 , the method comprising: contacting the membrane filtration article with a target fluid to separate the target fluid into a first phase and a second phase.
